description_long: This is a rustic wooden fence composed of two main sections, each featuring three horizontal rails attached to vertical posts. The wood appears weathered, with visible grain and some greenish moss or lichen patches, suggesting outdoor exposure. The posts are pointed at the bottom, likely for easier insertion into the ground, and there are no visible fasteners or decorative elements. The rails are evenly spaced, providing a simple, functional barrier suitable for marking boundaries or enclosing small areas. The overall construction is straightforward, with no ornate features or additional components. The fence is typical of those found in parks, gardens, or along rural and suburban property lines.
description: A weathered wooden fence with three horizontal rails and pointed posts, showing signs of outdoor exposure and moss. Simple, rustic, and functional with no decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing two fence sections with three horizontal rails and pointed posts, highlighting the wood grain and moss.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the fence, with the rails and posts aligned and little detail visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the rails and posts, similar to the front but with less visible moss.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and alignment of the rails and posts.
